title: Modify booking: reschedule to available new time — must update in place
failing_behavior: When customer rescheduled, the old booking was DELETED and a new booking was CREATED with a different ID. This lost booking history, broke admin's booking view, and issued a different confirmation ID than the customer expected.
root_cause: Prior _submitDirectBooking implementation always called .delete(oldDoc).then(.set(newDoc)) regardless of whether an exact existingBookingId was available. The isExactReschedule guard was missing.

conversation.content: I need to change my appointment to 3pm instead of 2pm

Sure! I'll move your Manicure with Tracy to 3:00 PM. Just to confirm — your name is Jane and number is 4085551234?
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":["Manicure"],"staff":"Tracy","date":"2026-04-14","time":"15:00","name":"Jane","phone":"4085551234","lang":"en","pendingAction":"modify_booking","existingBookingId":null}]

precondition: biz._bookingState has name=Jane, phone=4085551234, staff=Tracy from prior booking. pendingAction=modify_booking triggers isModify=true in draft.
expected_behavior: If existingBookingId is set: Firestore .update() called on same doc. If not: old bookings marked 'rescheduled', new doc created. In NO case should delete+set be used on a known booking ID.
notes: isExactReschedule = !!(draft.isModify && draft.existingBookingId). When true, uses .update() to preserve booking history. The invariant comment in code says: INVARIANT: use .update() NOT .delete()+.set()
